query: SELECT vt.slug, vt.label, vt.description, vt.category, COUNT(dvt.id) as doc_count FROM violation_tags vt LEFT JOIN document_violation_tags dvt ON vt.id = dvt.violation_tag_id GROUP BY vt.id ORDER BY doc_count DESC;
command: mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2N1WTErRdSjheBh.e46440e12186 --database 3yhU3DrKzf8Z5BNtvxatFF --execute SELECT vt.slug, vt.label, vt.description, vt.category, COUNT(dvt.id) as doc_count FROM violation_tags vt LEFT JOIN document_violation_tags dvt ON vt.id = dvt.violation_tag_id GROUP BY vt.id ORDER BY doc_count DESC;

rows.label: Due Process Defect
rows.description: Procedural or substantive due process violations under the Fifth or Fourteenth Amendment, including lack of notice, denial of opportunity to be heard, or fundamentally unfair proceedings.

rows.label: Speedy Trial / Delay
rows.description: Violations of the Sixth Amendment right to a speedy trial, including unexplained continuances, strategic delays, or failure to bring a defendant to trial within statutory timeframes. Barker v. Wingo, 407 U.S. 514 (1972).

rows.label: Faretta / Self-Representation Rights
rows.description: Issues related to a defendant's Sixth Amendment right to self-representation, including denial of Faretta hearings, inadequate colloquy, or coerced waiver of counsel. Faretta v. California, 422 U.S. 806 (1975).

rows.label: Access to Courts Interference
rows.description: Actions that obstruct a party's meaningful access to the courts, including denial of filing rights, interference with legal mail, restriction of self-represented litigant access, or retaliation for filing.

rows.label: Warrant or Bail Defect
rows.description: Defects in arrest warrants, search warrants, or bail determinations, including lack of probable cause, overbreadth, or excessive bail in violation of the Eighth Amendment.

rows.label: Prosecutorial Misconduct
rows.description: Improper conduct by a prosecutor including misrepresentation to the court, vouching for witnesses, improper argument, selective prosecution, or abuse of charging discretion.

rows.label: Judicial Disqualification / Bias
rows.description: Issues involving a judge's failure to recuse when required, appearance of partiality, ex parte communications, or conduct indicating bias against a party or class of litigants.

rows.label: Competency Proceeding Abuse
rows.description: Misuse of competency evaluation or restoration proceedings, including using competency as a delay tactic, failure to follow Drope/Pate standards, or improper commitment without adequate basis.

rows.label: Record Integrity Issue
rows.description: Concerns about the accuracy, completeness, or authenticity of the official court record, including missing transcripts, altered docket entries, or discrepancies between filed and recorded documents.

rows.label: First Amendment Retaliation
rows.description: Adverse government action taken in response to protected speech, petition, or association, including retaliatory prosecution, enhanced charges, or procedural retaliation for exercising legal rights.

rows.label: Elder or Caregiver Impact
rows.description: Cases where the defendant or affected party is an elder, caregiver, or person with a dependent, and where prosecution or institutional action has disproportionate collateral impact on vulnerable third parties.

rows.label: Public Records Noncompliance
rows.description: Failure to comply with public records request obligations, including untimely responses, improper denials, withholding of non-exempt records, or failure to maintain required records.

rows.label: Fourth Amendment / Search and Seizure
rows.description: Unlawful searches, seizures, or surveillance without valid warrant or recognized exception, including fruit of the poisonous tree issues and suppression hearing defects.

rows.label: Brady / Discovery Issue
rows.description: Failure to disclose exculpatory or impeachment evidence as required by Brady v. Maryland, 373 U.S. 83 (1963), Giglio v. United States, 405 U.S. 150 (1972), or applicable state discovery rules.

rows.label: Nunc Pro Tunc Concern
rows.description: Retroactive court orders or entries used to alter the record in ways that affect substantive rights, particularly where the retroactive entry is not supported by the original record.
